name: os-release

metadata.name: os-release
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: A module for reading systemd's os-release information on modern Linux distributions.

documentation:
# os-release

[![PyPI](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/os-release)](https://pypi.org/project/os-release)
[![Documentation Status](https://readthedocs.org/projects/python-os-release/badge/?version=latest)](https://python-os-release.readthedocs.io/en/latest/?badge=latest)

_os-release_ is a simple Python module for reading systemd's `os-release` information on modern Linux distributions.

It parses the contents of systemd's `os-release`
([os-release(5)](https://www.freedesktop.org/software/systemd/man/os-release.html)) files:
`/etc/os-release` or `/usr/lib/os-release`.

## Installation

_os-release_ is available on [PyPI](https://pypi.org/project/os-release).

Add it to your projects `setup.py` `install_requires`:

    install_requires=['os-release']

or install it directly via `pip`:

    $ pip install os-release

Alternatively, clone this Git repository and run:

    $ python setup.py install

## Documentation:

Documentation for _os-release_ is available on [Read the Docs](https://python-os-release.readthedocs.io/en/latest/).

classes.name: os_release.parser.OsReleaseParseException
documentation: Raised when the given `os-release` file or string cannot be parsed.

functions.name: os_release.current_release
documentation:
Return an {py:class}`OsRelease` tuple representing the contents of the `os-release` file for the current
operating system.

Tries to read the following files, in order (as per [os-release(5)]):

- `/etc/os-release`
- `/usr/lib/os-release`

```
raises FileNotFoundError

if no os-release file can be found.

raises OsReleaseParseException

if the os-release file cannot be parsed.
```

classes.name: os_release.OsRelease

methods.name: os_release.OsRelease.is_like
documentation:
Returns {py:const}`True` if the operating system represented by this {py:class}`OsRelease` has an
{py:attr}`id` or {py:attr}`id_like` that matches the passed {paramref}`query`.

```
param query

the id to test
```

class_variables.name: os_release.OsRelease.name
documentation:
---
substitutions:
  os-release#NAME: '`NAME`'
---

Equivalent to the [os-release#NAME] field in `os-release`.

As per [os-release(5)], this property will default to `Linux` if `NAME` is not present in `os-release`.

[os-release#name]: https://www.freedesktop.org/software/systemd/man/os-release.html#NAME=

class_variables.name: os_release.OsRelease.version
documentation:
---
substitutions:
  os-release#VERSION: '`VERSION`'
---

Equivalent to the [os-release#VERSION] field in `os-release`.

This property is optional and will default to {py:const}`None` if `VERSION` is not present in `os-release`.

[os-release#version]: https://www.freedesktop.org/software/systemd/man/os-release.html#VERSION=

class_variables.name: os_release.OsRelease.id
documentation:
---
substitutions:
  os-release#ID: '`ID`'
---

Equivalent to the [os-release#ID] field in `os-release`.

As per [os-release(5)], this property will default to `linux` if `ID` is not present in `os-release`.

[os-release#id]: https://www.freedesktop.org/software/systemd/man/os-release.html#ID=

class_variables.name: os_release.OsRelease.id_like
documentation:
---
substitutions:
  os-release#ID_LIKE: '`ID_LIKE`'
---

Equivalent to the [os-release#ID_LIKE] field in `os-release`.

The value of `ID_LIKE` is automatically split on whitespace into a tuple.

This property is optional and will default to an empty tuple if `ID_LIKE` is not present in `os-release`.

[os-release#id_like]: https://www.freedesktop.org/software/systemd/man/os-release.html#ID_LIKE=

class_variables.name: os_release.OsRelease.urls
documentation:
See {py:class}`OsRelease.Urls`.

This property will always be an instance of {py:class}`OsRelease.Urls`, even if no `*_URL` fields are present
in `os-release`.

class_variables.name: os_release.OsRelease.vendor_extra
documentation: A dict of vendor-specific fields (fields that are unknown to `os-release`).

class_variables.name: os_release.OsRelease.Urls.privacy_policy
documentation: Equivalent to the `PRIVACY_POLICY_URL` field in `os-release`.
Represents the various `*_URL` fields in `os-release`.

Represents the fields of a systemd `os-release` file.

{py:class}`OsRelease` is a {py:class}`typing.NamedTuple`.
